description.en: A round Catalan country loaf that skips scoring entirely: bakers flip it seam-up before its final rise, so the dough bursts open along its own folds into deep, rustic cracks. A solid starter and 60-70% hydration give it big, irregular holes and real sourdough tang.
description.de: Ein rundes katalanisches Landbrot ohne jedes Einschneiden: Vor der letzten Gare wird es mit der Naht nach oben gelegt, sodass der Teig entlang seiner Falten in tiefe, rustikale Risse aufplatzt. Fester Sauerteig und 60-70% Hydration sorgen für große, unregelmäßige Poren.
description.es: Una hogaza redonda catalana que prescinde del greñado: los panaderos la colocan boca abajo antes de la última fermentación, para que la masa reviente a lo largo de sus propios pliegues en grietas rústicas. Una masa madre sólida y 60-70% de hidratación dan grandes alvéolos.
description.fr: Une miche ronde catalane qui se passe complètement du grignage: les boulangers la retournent avant la dernière pousse pour que la pâte éclate le long de ses plis en fissures rustiques. Un levain ferme et 60-70% d'hydratation donnent de grands alvéoles irréguliers et un vrai peps.

origin.note: One of Spain's IGP-recognized breads; the round hogaza needs no scoring because the seams left from shaping crack open on their own during baking.

yield.shape: round loaf (hogaza), unscored, it cracks naturally

keeping.note: The sourdough and thick crust give it decent keeping power, typical of Catalonia's classic everyday country loaf.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine flour, water, starter and salt into a smooth, cohesive dough.

method.title: Bulk ferment
method.summary: Let rise until doubled, about 3 hours at room temperature.

method.title: Shape without scoring
method.summary: Shape into a round loaf and rest 10 minutes; no slashing is needed for this bread.

method.title: Flip the seams up
method.summary: Set the dough seam-side up for its final proof so the seams crack open naturally in the oven instead of a scored top.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Let it rise again until doubled.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake starting hot around 200°C, then lower to about 165°C, roughly 40 minutes total, until deeply golden and the natural seams have burst into a rustic crust.

source.adaptation: Reconstructed using this site's no-scoring, flip-to-crack method with typical pan de payés ratios (60-70% hydration, 15-20% solid starter), not the site's own commercial starter-packet amounts.
